content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

August 3, 2010

Tommy Owens
1816 Lyon Street
San Francisco, California 94115

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-10-119

Dear Mr. Owens:

This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the campaign provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Please note that our advice is based solely on the Act. We
therefore offer no opinion on the application, if any, of other laws.

QUESTION

May a committee for election of a candidate for state office use the names, addresses and
contribution data of donors to a candidate for federal office for the purpose of mailing campaign
materials to these donors if the information is obtained from a publicly available website.

CONCLUSION

The Act does not prohibit such a mailing. However, if over two hundred substantially
similar pieces of mail are sent within a calendar month, the mailing constitutes a "mass mailing"
under the Act and certain requirements must be met, including a requirement that certain
information must appear on the outside of each piece of mail and on at least one of the inserts.

FACTS

You are the campaign manager for the committee "Alfonso Faustino for Assembly.² You
would like to mail campaign materials urging support for Mr. Faustino's candidacy, including a

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

² A person or combination of persons becomes a committee upon (i) receiving contributions totaling
$1,000 or more in a calendar year, (ii) making independent expenditures totaling $1,000 or more in a calendar year,

solicitation for contributions. You intend to obtain information needed for this mailing from a
list of donors to another campaign appearing on a website, www.fundrace.huffingtonpost.com.

The website was created for use in a federal presidential race. In your mailing, you
intend to state that you have learned of the recipient's contribution to past candidacies from an
internet posting. You also intend to include a fundraising letter, your business card, a small
brochure and a fundraising envelope.

ANALYSIS

Nothing in the Act prohibits the use of any particular source for obtaining information to be used
for election related activity. The Act, and regulations adopted under the Act, governs, among other
things, the reporting of contributions received and expenditures made by a committee, the establishment
of bank accounts, recordkeeping, loan limits, treatment of outstanding debts, restrictions on
contributions to other candidates and committees, limitations on the activities of lobbyists and the
content of written solicitations for contributions.

Your inquiry implicates the mass mailing³ provisions of the Act. Section 84305 requires, among
other things, that a mass mailing contain the name, street address and city of the candidate or committee
on the outside of each piece of mail and on at least one side of the inserts included within each of the
mailings. The disclosures must be in no less than 6-point type and in a color or print that contrasts with
the background, so as to be easily legible. Regulations 18435 and 18421.2 set forth additional
requirements. We urge you to familiarize yourself with the mass mailing requirements of the Act and
related regulations.⁴

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel

By: Valentina Joyce
Counsel, Legal Division

Enclosure

or (iii) making contributions totaling $10,000 or more in a calendar year to or at the behest of candidates or
committees. (Section 82013.) For purposes of our analysis, we will assume that "Alfonso Faustino for Assembly" is
a committee.

³ A "mass mailing" means over 200 substantially similar pieces of mail sent during a calendar month.
(Section 82041.5; Regulation 18435.)

⁴ We are enclosing a page from the Commission's Campaign Manual that summarizes the requirements for
mailings. The entire manual is available on the commission's website: www.fppc.ca.gov.
